author: anon-ab6221f8
content: Shipped in 606c8e8a. Root cause: crosslink init --force short-circuits the 'Initializing database' step when .crosslink/.hub-cache/ already exists — exits 0 but leaves issues.db missing. Happened to ~half the track-all clones due to timing between the poll loop's ensure_hub_cache_worktree and the init subprocess. Fix: wipe_partial_crosslink_state now removes the hub-cache worktree properly (git worktree remove --force) AND nukes .crosslink/ entirely, so crosslink init sees a truly clean slate on retry. Hub-cache regenerates on the next poll tick via ensure_hub_cache_worktree. All 6 previously-broken repos retroactively initialised via POST /w/<slug>/init; all 15 tracked projects now write_capability=ready.
